stages.summary: Go to the living room and join Mom.
stages.beat_note: Ella still needs to join her mother in the living room.

stages.summary: Tell Mom you're up for a movie night.
stages.beat_note: Ella is deciding whether to say yes to a movie night with her mother.
stages.update_message: Objective updated: Tell Mom you're up for a movie night.

stages.summary: Pick a movie from Mom's list.
stages.beat_note: Mom has offered a few movies, and Ella needs to pick one.
stages.update_message: Objective updated: Pick a movie from Mom's list.

stages.summary: Dad is bringing fruit over. Wait for him to join you before the movie starts.
stages.beat_note: Dad is still in the kitchen pulling together fruit before he joins the movie night in the living room.
stages.update_message: Objective updated: Dad is bringing fruit over. Wait for him to join you before the movie starts.

stages.summary: Movie night wrapped up with {movie_title}. Stay with the family for a bit.
stages.beat_note: The family has just finished watching {movie_title} together and is lingering in the afterglow.
stages.update_message: Objective updated: Movie night wrapped up with {movie_title}. Stay with the family for a bit.
narrative_lines: The lights go low, the projector hums, and {movie_title} washes the living room wall in flickering color.
Every now and then one of them leans forward to point at the wall, then settles back before the moment can turn into commentary.
Halfway through, Mom starts laughing about old weekend rentals and the cheap DVDs they used to borrow from the corner store.
Mom and Dad end up laughing about the first movie they ever took Ella to see, because she fell asleep almost immediately and slept through the entire thing.
By the time the movie ends, the room feels warmer. The three of them have wandered into a pocket of shared memory that makes tomorrow seem farther away for a little while.
Dad: Give me a minute. I should get these fruit dishes to the sink before they start sticking.

stages.summary: Go to the kitchen and check in with Dad.
stages.beat_note: Ella still needs to go to the kitchen and check in about a late-night snack.
stages.update_message: Objective updated: Go to the kitchen and check in with Dad.

stages.summary: Talk to Dad about a late-night snack.
stages.beat_note: Ella is checking in about a quick late-night snack in the kitchen.
stages.update_message: Objective updated: Talk to Dad about a late-night snack.

stages.summary: Pick a late-night snack to make with Dad.
stages.beat_note: Ella has a few quick late-night snack options and needs to pick one.
stages.update_message: Objective updated: Pick a late-night snack to make with Dad.

stages.summary: You and Dad just made {snack_title} together. Stay with him for a bit.
stages.beat_note: The kitchen still smells like {snack_title}, and the two of you are lingering in the shared memory of making it together.
stages.update_message: Objective updated: You and Dad just made {snack_title} together. Stay with him for a bit.

stages.summary: Go back to your room and get ready for bed.
stages.beat_note: The last thing left tonight is heading back to her room and letting the night wind down.
Mom and Dad come by to say good night before Ella leaves in the morning.
stages.next_chapter_preview: Tomorrow is departure day. The next chapter begins with the morning goodbye, the final small routines, and the moment leaving becomes real.
narrative_lines: A few minutes later, Mom taps softly on the doorframe and Dad appears just behind her.
Mom crosses the room first, cups Ella's face for a second like she is memorizing it, and kisses her good night.
Dad leans down to press a kiss to the top of Ella's head. "Get some sleep, kiddo. Morning comes fast."
Mom smiles, damp-eyed but steady. "We're so proud of you. Tomorrow can be big without you having to hold all of it at once."
Dad gives Ella's shoulder a squeeze. "Your room is still your room. That part doesn't change."
They linger for one last second, then leave her with the kind of quiet that only settles over a house after everyone has finally said what they needed to say.
